evidence-binding: n1-fix ef098cd790b4
<!-- RED: paste RED here -->
RED: testGitlab/GiteaAdaptiveNodeOperatorHint445 leaks orient scratch into the repo (pre-impl). Repro of the current spawn (no cwd) from the worktree: `KAOLA_WORKFLOW_OFFLINE=1 node kaola-{gl,gt}-workflow-adaptive-node.js orient --project nonexistent-{gl,gt}-445-test --json --summary` emitted the sentinel `summary: refuse | reason: plan_missing ...` BUT left untracked scratch in the working tree — `git status --porcelain` showed `?? kaola-workflow/nonexistent-gl-445-test/` and `?? kaola-workflow/nonexistent-gt-445-test/`, each containing `.cache/orient-envelope.json`. The "read-only" orient materialized a project dir in the repo cwd that the chain never cleans up.
<!-- GREEN: paste GREEN here -->
GREEN: After redirecting each --summary spawn to a `tempRoot('kw-{gl,gt}-orient-summary-')` cwd inside try/finally (fs.rmSync in finally): `node plugins/kaola-workflow-gitlab/scripts/test-gitlab-workflow-scripts.js` → exit 0, `testGitlabAdaptiveNodeOperatorHint445 (#445/#446): PASSED`; `node .../test-gitea-workflow-scripts.js` → exit 0, `testGiteaAdaptiveNodeOperatorHint445 (#445/#446): PASSED` — the preserved `summaryOut.startsWith('summary:')` sentinel assertion still green in both. `git status --porcelain` afterward shows ONLY the two write-set file edits (no `nonexistent-{gl,gt}-445-test`); `find` for `nonexistent-g*-445-test` returns empty. Both edition chains green: `npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitlab` exit 0 ("GitLab workflow walkthrough simulation passed", edition-sync --check passed) and `npm run test:kaola-workflow:gitea` exit 0 ("Gitea workflow walkthrough simulation passed"), with no scratch leak after either chain.
